title: saldinia longistipulata
intro: Saldinia longistipulata, also known as the Himalayan sida or Himalayan joja, is a plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, native to the Himalayan region. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems, as well as to aid digestion and reduce stress. Saldinia longistipulata can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic and Unani medicine preparations.
